comments.text:
## Problem

Bedrock.DataPlane.Demux.ShardServerTest 'flushes when version gap exceeded' test intermittently fails when run as part of the full test suite, but passes when run in isolation.

## Symptoms

```
1) test flushing flushes when version gap exceeded (Bedrock.DataPlane.Demux.ShardServerTest)
   test/bedrock/data_plane/demux/shard_server_test.exs:134
   Assertion failed, no matching message after 0ms
   The process mailbox is empty.
   code: assert_received {:durable, 99, durable_version}
```

## Root Cause

Timing-dependent test. The test uses :timer.sleep(50) to wait for the flush operation to complete and send the durability message. Under load (when running full test suite), 50ms may not be sufficient.

## File Location

test/bedrock/data_plane/demux/shard_server_test.exs:134

## Suggested Fix

Replace :timer.sleep with assert_receive with a longer timeout:

```elixir
# Instead of:
:timer.sleep(50)
assert_received {:durable, 99, durable_version}

# Use:
assert_receive {:durable, 99, durable_version}, 1000
```
comments.author: Jason Allum

title: Fix flaky ShardServerTest - flushing test timeout
